reviewNotes: Source-level review complete for the obligatory-frame cluster. The Gettysburg Address and Second Inaugural provide documented primary-source grounding for Lincoln's construction of the Civil War as a test to be endured rather than a victory to be exploited. Both sources are confirmed in the PPW bibliography and american-civil-war-union source-pack. Advancing to source-review status; not yet ready for promoted-finding because passage-level anchoring and inter-annotator reliability checks remain outstanding (see promotionBlockers).
